pr.title: Match and improve several source functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Batch PR with independent source-matching improvements across Ice Mountain ground code, sysdolphin particles, Dolphin THP decode, fighter item handling, and menu count sorting. The PR body reports configure --require-protos, report build, and ninja verification; the bot report records two new 100% matches and three unmatched-item score improvements.

key_files.role: Matched __THPRestartDefinition by introducing a local THPRestartFields overlay for restart-specific fields and using it for RST, nMCU, and currMCU writes.
Matched hsd_80398C04 by replacing pointer arithmetic with hsd_804D0908 indexing and correcting particle color/target field initialization.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report shows main/sysdolphin/baselib/particle hsd_80398C04 99.64% -> 100.00%

key_files.role: Improved grIceMt_801F9ACC by changing return type to int, returning did/0, using GET_GROUND instead of user_data access, widening id to s32, adding PAD_STACK(16), and introducing a pointer temporary for gv.icemt2.xC8.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: improve grIceMt_801F9ACC by tightening the Ice Mountain data/ID handling
text_corpus: bot report shows main/melee/gr/gricemt grIceMt_801F9ACC 96.34% -> 98.90%

key_files.role: Updated grIceMt_801F9ACC prototype from void to int to match the source body change.
Improved ft_800895E0 by removing a temporary item GObj local and directly reusing fp->item_gobj in the null check and it_8026B6C8 call.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: improve ft_800895E0 by reusing the item GObj load shape
text_corpus: bot report shows main/melee/ft/ft_0892 ft_800895E0 98.86% -> 99.98%

key_files.role: Improved mnCount_8025092C by moving CountEntry tmp declaration out of the swap block, removing PAD_STACK(8), and reversing the row comparison condition shape.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: improve mnCount_8025092C by reshaping the row comparison
text_corpus: bot report shows main/melee/mn/mncount mnCount_8025092C 99.38% -> 99.92%

what_changed: THPDec.c: added THPRestartFields with pad[0x8FC], nMCU, currMCU, and RST; __THPRestartDefinition casts THPFileInfo* to that overlay for restart field stores.

particle.c: removed a prior TODO about a partial match, changed the default particle slot from hsd_804D08E8 pointer arithmetic to &hsd_804D0908[linkNo], initialized primCol to 0xFF instead of envCol, zeroed envCol instead of envColTarget, and set rotateTarget to 0.0F instead of sizeTarget.

gricemt.c/h: grIceMt_801F9ACC now returns int, returns 0 for early invalid segment IDs, returns did at the end, updates the header prototype, switches many Ground_GObj user_data reads to GET_GROUND(), and includes stack/local-shape changes.
ft_0892.c: ft_800895E0 removes Item_GObj* temp and directly checks fp->item_gobj before passing it to it_8026B6C8.
mncount.c: mnCount_8025092C changes local declaration placement and comparison expression shape while removing PAD_STACK(8).

Verification evidence in the PR body lists python configure.py --require-protos, ninja build/GALE01/report.json, and ninja; the bot report records +844 matched-code bytes, two new matches, and three improvements.

smart_moves.text: Kept the header synchronized with the recovered grIceMt_801F9ACC return type instead of only changing the C body.
Responded to review by replacing a proposed local user_data helper with the existing GET_GROUND() helper in grIceMt_801F9ACC.
evidence_refs: review_comments: jurrejelle asked can this use an existing GET_GROUND()?
review_comments: author said removed the local user_data helper and switched call sites to GET_GROUND()

smart_moves.text: Used a named existing array symbol hsd_804D0908[linkNo] instead of arithmetic off hsd_804D08E8 for particle list slot selection.
For ft_800895E0, the final shape directly reuses fp->item_gobj and removes an extra local, matching the PR body's stated item GObj load-shape goal.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: improve ft_800895E0 by reusing the item GObj load shape

decomp_lessons.lesson: When review finds a subsystem accessor that expresses a typed object retrieval, prefer that existing helper over a new one-off local helper or raw user_data access, provided the final diff supports the helper use.
evidence_refs: review_comments: can this use an existing GET_GROUND()?
review_comments: removed the local user_data helper and switched the call sites in grIceMt_801F9ACC to GET_GROUND()

decomp_lessons.lesson: A recovered function body can expose a real return value; update both early returns and the final return, and keep the owning header prototype aligned.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grIceMt_801F9ACC void -> int and return did

decomp_lessons.lesson: For matching small field-access mismatches, a temporary overlay struct can be used to express fields at a known layout offset when the full public struct access shape does not match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: THPRestartFields pad[0x8FC], nMCU, currMCU, RST

decomp_lessons.lesson: Declaration placement and comparison operand order can matter for near-matching selection/insertion-style loops; here moving tmp out of the block and changing entries[min].val < entries[j].val improved mnCount_8025092C.

naming_conventions.name: THPRestartFields
naming_conventions.convention: Temporary/local overlay struct name describes the restart-definition field subset rather than inventing a full replacement for THPFileInfo.

naming_conventions.name: GET_GROUND
naming_conventions.convention: Use the established ground accessor macro/helper for retrieving Ground* from a Ground_GObj/HSD_GObj rather than a file-local grIceMt_GetUserData helper.
evidence_refs: review_comments: removed the local user_data helper

naming_conventions.name: new_var
naming_conventions.convention: The diff introduces a non-semantic temporary name new_var for pointer-shape matching; PR evidence does not establish it as a naming standard.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use an overlay struct cast to shift stores from THPFileInfo field syntax to restart-specific layout fields.
Use a direct existing global-array symbol index instead of pointer arithmetic off a nearby base symbol to resolve relocation differences in a particle function.
Correcting field names that share similar layout neighborhoods can close a match when the generated or prior source used the wrong color/target field.
A narrow PAD_STACK insertion remained in grIceMt_801F9ACC while a PAD_STACK was removed from mnCount_8025092C; stack padding use was function-specific rather than treated as general style.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report shows both functions improved but not fully matched

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Directly reusing a struct field expression at both the null check and call site can match better than caching it in a local.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: reusing the item GObj load shape

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether the new local user_data helper could use an existing GET_GROUND().
review_feedback.resolution: Author replied that the latest push removed the local user_data helper and switched grIceMt_801F9ACC call sites to GET_GROUND(); the final diff shows GET_GROUND uses.

follow_up_queries: grIceMt_801F9ACC void to int return did header prototype

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, changed files, counts
raw_diff: full diff for six changed files
text_corpus: bot report with matching percentages
review_comments: GET_GROUND review and author resolution
notes: The slice includes a complete diff and text corpus for the PR, plus review comments and bot report.
Verification commands are reported by the PR body; no independent build logs are loaded beyond the bot report excerpt.
The bot report supports match/improvement percentages, but the intake does not independently rerun objdiff or builds.
The diff shows PAD_STACK and a non-semantic new_var in grIceMt_801F9ACC; there is not enough evidence to generalize those as preferred style.
The PR was merged according to metadata, but acceptance of broader standards should not be inferred beyond this PR's review outcome.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer existing ground accessor over local user_data helper when review identifies it
accepted_candidate_records.text: In grIceMt_801F9ACC, review asked whether a proposed local user_data helper could use existing GET_GROUND(); the author removed the local helper and switched the call sites to GET_GROUND(). This is a graph-safe example of using an established subsystem accessor when it expresses the same object retrieval.
evidence_refs: review_comments: can this use an existing GET_GROUND()?
review_comments: removed the local user_data helper and switched the call sites in grIceMt_801F9ACC to GET_GROUND()

accepted_candidate_records.title: Function signature recovery should update owning header
accepted_candidate_records.text: grIceMt_801F9ACC changed from void to int in the source and the gricemt.h prototype was updated in the same PR. The body now returns 0 on the early invalid-segment path and returns did at the end.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grIceMt_801F9ACC body return type and returns

source_update_candidates.title: Existing accessors should be considered before adding one-off helpers
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2582 provides review evidence that a local helper for ground user_data access was removed in favor of existing GET_GROUND() call sites. This may be a useful example under typed-fields/accessor standards, but should be curated as an example rather than a new universal rule.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is supported by explicit review feedback and final diff, but should be reviewed for whether current standards already cover it.

source_update_candidates.title: Overlay structs can be narrow matching bridges when full struct syntax mismatches
source_update_candidates.text: THPDec.c introduced THPRestartFields as a local overlay over THPFileInfo to match __THPRestartDefinition. If curated, this should be framed narrowly: overlay structs need layout evidence and verification, and should not become a replacement for recovering real owned struct fields.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful matching tactic, but potentially conflicts with preferring real typed fields; curator should decide whether to document as a constrained bridge pattern.

source_update_candidates.title: gricemt.h owns grIceMt_801F9ACC prototype
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2582 updates the grIceMt_801F9ACC declaration in src/melee/gr/gricemt.h when the body in src/melee/gr/gricemt.c changes return type from void to int.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact about owning header for this symbol; current source should be checked before adding.

rejection_notes: Do not promote PAD_STACK(16) in grIceMt_801F9ACC as a general tactic without narrower verifier evidence; the same PR removes PAD_STACK(8) from mnCount_8025092C.
Do not treat the variable name new_var as an accepted naming convention; it appears to be a local matching artifact with no semantic review support.
Do not infer that all user_data accesses should become GET_GROUND(); the evidence is limited to the reviewed grIceMt_801F9ACC call sites.
Do not claim grIceMt_801F9ACC is matched; the bot report only shows improvement from 96.34% to 98.90%.
Do not claim ft_800895E0 or mnCount_8025092C are fully matched; the bot report shows 99.98% and 99.92%, respectively.
